documentation.description:
=====================================================================
Vk_Safe  --  A SPARK-verified safety layer over the raw Vk binding.
=====================================================================

The thin binding (package Vk) is the raw C ABI -- unsafe by nature, all
System.Address. This layer adds what no Vulkan binding anywhere has:
PROOF of correct API usage. A Safe_Instance cannot be enumerated or
destroyed unless it is live, and cannot be touched after it is destroyed
-- and these are not runtime checks but PROOF OBLIGATIONS. gnatprove
rejects the misuse at build time. That is the moat: a Vulkan you cannot
crash by misusing it (ash is `unsafe`; vulkano is safe-but-unproven).

Pattern: the visible part is SPARK (the verified contract a client must
satisfy); the private part and body are the trusted C-interop glue.
=====================================================================
